title: Syzygium crebrinerve
taxonomy: Syzygium crebrinerve is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae. It belongs to the genus Syzygium, which comprises over 1,000 species of trees and shrubs. The Myrtaceae family is a large and diverse group of plants, including eucalyptus, guava, and clove trees. The plant is native to Australia, where it is commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. Syzygium crebrinerve is a deciduous tree that grows up to 10 meters tall.
